name: preserves evidence-backed causal chain for confirmed bug
description: Real GitHub issue fixture with a detailed, evidence-backed root cause. The diagnosis must preserve the causal chain instead of merely classifying the report as a bug.

issue.title: GitHub writes fail silently in resource-event-triggered turns
issue.author: dcramer
issue.body:
Resource-event-triggered agent turns run with a system actor credential context, but GitHub user-write grants require a currentUser or credentialSubject token slot derived from a real user identity. When a subscription event fires and Junior attempts a write, neither slot is populated, so the credential is rejected. Retrying the same action as a new user turn succeeds immediately.

## Root cause (high confidence)

reply-executor.ts assigns resource events `{ actor: { platform: "system", name: "resource-event" } }`. That context flows into the sandbox egress token through tools.ts. credential-hooks.ts only creates currentUser when the actor has a user shape, so the system actor produces no currentUser token slot. The GitHub credential issuer then rejects user-write because both currentUser and credentialSubject are absent.

## Broken flows

- Resource-event-triggered GitHub writes
- Other system-actor turns attempting sandbox egress writes

## Not broken

- Continuation turns that restore the user actor
- Read-only operations using installation-read grants

## Fix direction

Either deny writes from resource-event turns with an actionable explanation, or persist the subscription-initiating user as a credentialSubject and authorize event-triggered writes on that user's behalf.

pass: Preserves the reported system actor to missing currentUser or credentialSubject to rejected GitHub write causal chain.
Distinguishes reporter-provided evidence from independently verified source or command evidence.
Provides a useful fix direction and validation plan without claiming a reproduction or checkout inspection.
Avoids a public restatement because the trusted reporter already supplied a useful root cause analysis.
fail: Claims the bug was reproduced or source files were inspected.
Invents source locations or evidence not present in the issue.
